body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: OR tiered cascade fires in live dispatch
body.intention: I intend that inference dispatch observably routes through analyst→worker→minion tiers before hitting the CC block.
body.description:
D-or-tiered-cascade-2026-06-02 shipped T-inference-tiered-fallback and T-models-registry-refresh (both closed), but no consequence check was filed.

**What changed:** OR inference dispatch restructured as tiered cascade: analyst tier → worker tier → minion tier, with CC block only as last resort.

**Predicted unintended effects:** Tiered fallback may exist in code but not be invoked — Granny may still be routing directly to CC for all tasks. Registry refresh may have updated model names without connecting them to the tier selector.

**Signals to watch:** Watch a Granny dispatch cycle — does the channel log show analyst/worker/minion tiers being attempted before CC? Is the model registry queried at dispatch time or cached from startup?

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-23

**Affected files:** None — observation and channel log review
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe live dispatch and verify tier progression in channel logs. Out of scope: model quality, cost analysis.
**Test plan:** no tests — live observation ticket
**Completion criteria:** Observe one Granny dispatch cycle in channel log; verify tier progression is visible (analyst attempted first, fallback to worker/minion if analyst fails). Record: working / not firing / partial.
